figures.caption: FIG. 2: Second-derivative profiles with respect to x at multiple time snapshots. Dashed: analytical solution.
FIG. 4: Spatial derivative ∂u/∂x at multiple time snapshots. Dashed: numerical reference.

TABLE IX: Sensitivity of DC-PINNs on the Navier–Stokes equation. Reported values are RMSE in logarithmic scale, evaluated on dense grids and expressed as (L0,Lf ,Lh, µ1/µ2), where L0 = max{L0u ,L0v ,L0p},

TABLE VII: Sensitivity of DC-PINNs on the Heat problem. Reported values are RMSE in log-scale, given as (L0,Lf ,Lb,L(h1+h2)) in dense evaluation grids. Bold entries indicate the overall top-4 choices (by the sum of the four metrics; more negative is better).

TABLE VIII: Sensitivity of DC-PINNs on the volatility surface calibration. The reported values are RMSE on the logarithmic scale, given as (L0,Lb,Lf,Lh(x+ xx+ t)) on dense evaluation grids. The bold entries indicate the overall top-4 configurations, and the lower values correspond to better performance.
TABLE III: Validation (RMSE: bold is best) for the volatility surface calibration.
